plan.title: Observability Fourth Pass: Thread, Wire, Verify
plan.architecture:
SegmentationTrainer.fit() |--- log_epoch_complete() ---> events.jsonl + heartbeat.json |--- GpuHeartbeatMonitor -----> heartbeat.json (background thread) \--- [OTel span per epoch] ---> Grafana Tempo (via LGTM)
flow_observability_context() |--- JSONL flow_start/flow_end -> events.jsonl |--- [OTel span per flow] -----> Grafana Tempo (via LGTM) \--- prefect_hooks @task -------> Python logger (Prefect UI)
Docker HEALTHCHECK (per service) |--- GPU flows: heartbeat.json staleness (healthcheck_training.py) |--- CPU batch flows: events.jsonl staleness (healthcheck_cpu.py) \--- Long-running services: HTTP /health endpoint
Dashboard Flow (observability consumer) |--- reads heartbeat.json for training status |--- reads events.jsonl for progress summary \--- includes stall detection for active MLflow runs

phase.title: Thread event_logger into SegmentationTrainer epoch loop
phase.description:
StructuredEventLogger.log_epoch_complete() has been tested and working since pass 2, but the SegmentationTrainer never receives or calls it. The event_logger from gpu_flow_observability_context is captured at train_flow.py:1467 but never passed down through train_one_fold_task to the trainer.
This phase adds event_logger as an optional parameter to SegmentationTrainer and wires the call into the epoch loop inside fit(), then passes it from the flow context through the task to the trainer constructor.

task.title: Add event_logger parameter to SegmentationTrainer
task.description:
Modify SegmentationTrainer.__init__() to accept an optional event_logger parameter of type StructuredEventLogger | None (default None). Store it as self._event_logger.
In the fit() method epoch loop (after line ~785 where epoch_wall_time is computed and all metrics are available), add the log_epoch_complete() call:
# Structured JSONL epoch logging (pass 4 — G1 fix) if self._event_logger is not None: gpu_snap = {} if self._monitor is not None: snap = self._monitor.get_latest_snapshot() if snap is not None: gpu_snap = { "gpu_util_pct": snap.gpu_utilization_percent, "gpu_memory_used_mb": snap.gpu_memory_used_mb, } self._event_logger.log_epoch_complete( epoch=epoch + 1, max_epochs=self.config.max_epochs, train_loss=train_result.loss, val_loss=val_result.loss, val_dice=val_result.metrics.get("val/dice", 0.0), lr=current_lr, epoch_wall_s=epoch_wall_time, gpu_util_pct=gpu_snap.get("gpu_util_pct", 0), vram_used_mb=gpu_snap.get("gpu_memory_used_mb", 0), )
Also add log_training_start() before the epoch loop and log_training_end() after the loop completes (or on exception). The model_family comes from self._get_model_family(), loss_name from the config, etc.
IMPORTANT: The event_logger parameter must use TYPE_CHECKING import guard to avoid circular import (structured_logging imports nothing from pipeline, but defensive coding).
task.acceptance: SegmentationTrainer accepts event_logger=StructuredEventLogger(...). After fit() runs, events.jsonl contains training_start, epoch_complete (one per epoch), and training_end events. heartbeat.json is updated each epoch with current progress.

task.title: Thread event_logger from train_flow through to trainer
task.description:
The wiring chain: train_flow.py captures event_logger from gpu_flow_observability_context (line 1467), but the current call to training_subflow and then train_one_fold_task does not pass it through.
Strategy: Pass event_logger through the config dict as a special key '_event_logger' that train_one_fold_task extracts and passes to SegmentationTrainer. This avoids changing the Prefect @task signature (Prefect serializes task parameters — StructuredEventLogger is not serializable).
Alternative (preferred if Prefect allows): Use Prefect runtime context or a module-level variable set by the flow before calling the task. Since all tasks run in the same process (no remote workers for single-machine Docker), a module-level threading.local() is safe:
# In train_flow.py, after context manager yields: import threading _flow_context = threading.local()
# Inside flow body: _flow_context.event_logger = event_logger
# In train_one_fold_task: _event_logger = getattr(_flow_context, 'event_logger', None) trainer = SegmentationTrainer( model, training_config, ..., event_logger=_event_logger, )
This pattern is used because Prefect @task parameters must be JSON-serializable, and StructuredEventLogger is not.
Also wire hpo_flow.py similarly — HPO trials construct SegmentationTrainer instances that need epoch logging.
task.acceptance: When train_flow runs, the SegmentationTrainer receives the event_logger and produces epoch-level JSONL events. The events.jsonl file contains flow_start, training_start, epoch_complete (per epoch), training_end, and flow_end in chronological order. No Prefect serialization errors.

task.title: Behavioral test: epoch events appear in events.jsonl
task.description:
Write a behavioral test that constructs a minimal SegmentationTrainer with a real StructuredEventLogger (writing to tmp_path), runs fit() for 2 epochs with tiny mock data (4x4x4 volumes), and asserts events.jsonl contains the expected event sequence.
This test uses @pytest.mark.model_loading (it instantiates DynUNet) so it is excluded from staging tier but included in prod tier. A lightweight variant using a mock model (just returns input) runs in staging tier.
Expected event sequence in events.jsonl: 1. training_start (model_family, loss_name, num_folds, max_epochs) 2. epoch_complete (epoch=1, train_loss, val_loss, val_dice, lr, epoch_wall_s) 3. epoch_complete (epoch=2, ...) 4. training_end (status=completed, total_wall_s, folds_completed)
Also verify heartbeat.json has been updated with epoch=2 and eta_s.
task.acceptance: Staging-tier test (mock model) passes: events.jsonl has training_start + 2x epoch_complete + training_end. heartbeat.json updated. Prod-tier test (real DynUNet) also passes.

phase.title: Docker HEALTHCHECK for all flow services
phase.description:
Only the train service has a HEALTHCHECK (healthcheck_training.py, which reads heartbeat.json). The other 14 flow services have no health monitoring.
Strategy by service type:
- GPU batch flows (hpo, post_training, analyze): Reuse healthcheck_training.py (they have GpuHeartbeatMonitor writing heartbeat.json via gpu_flow_observability_context).
- CPU batch flows (data, biostatistics, acquisition, deploy, annotation, drift_simulation, synthetic_generation, qa, maintenance, pipeline): Create healthcheck_cpu.py that checks events.jsonl staleness instead of heartbeat.json (CPU flows write events via flow_observability_context but do NOT have heartbeat).
- Long-running services (dashboard-api, dashboard-ui, monai-label): Use HTTP health endpoint checks (curl-based).
- Dashboard batch flow: Use healthcheck_cpu.py.

task.title: Create healthcheck_cpu.py for CPU flow services
task.description:
Create scripts/healthcheck_cpu.py mirroring healthcheck_training.py but checking events.jsonl staleness instead of heartbeat.json:
1. Read LOGS_DIR/events.jsonl 2. Parse the last line (most recent event) 3. Check timestamp staleness against STALL_THRESHOLD_MINUTES 4. Grace period for startup (HEALTH_GRACE_PERIOD_MINUTES) 5. Exit 0 (healthy) or 1 (unhealthy)
Uses ONLY stdlib (no minivess imports). Same env vars as healthcheck_training.py for consistency.
Edge case: events.jsonl may not exist yet during flow startup (before flow_observability_context is entered). Grace period handles this.
task.acceptance: healthcheck_cpu.py returns exit 0 when events.jsonl has a recent event, exit 1 when stale or missing (after grace period). Test verifies both healthy and unhealthy paths with fresh/stale/missing events.jsonl.

task.title: Add HEALTHCHECK to all flow services in docker-compose.flows.yml
task.description:
Add healthcheck directives to all flow services that currently lack them.
GPU batch flows (hpo, post_training, analyze, gpu-benchmark):
healthcheck: test: ["CMD", "python", "scripts/healthcheck_training.py"] interval: 60s timeout: 10s retries: 3 start_period: 600s
CPU batch flows (data, biostatistics, acquisition, deploy, annotation, drift_simulation, synthetic_generation, pipeline, maintenance, dashboard):
healthcheck: test: ["CMD", "python", "scripts/healthcheck_cpu.py"] interval: 60s timeout: 10s retries: 5 start_period: 300s
Long-running HTTP services (dashboard-api):
healthcheck: test: ["CMD-SHELL", "curl -sf http://localhost:${DASHBOARD_API_PORT:-8090}/health || exit 1"] interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 3 start_period: 60s
dashboard-ui (nginx):
healthcheck: test: ["CMD-SHELL", "curl -sf http://localhost:80/ || exit 1"] interval: 30s timeout: 5s retries: 3 start_period: 30s
monai-label:
healthcheck: test: ["CMD-SHELL", "curl -sf http://localhost:${MONAI_LABEL_PORT:-8000}/info || exit 1"] interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 3 start_period: 120s
IMPORTANT: This modifies docker-compose.flows.yml which is a declarative config. Per CLAUDE.md Rule #31, we only add healthcheck keys (new keys, not modifying existing). The healthcheck values are deterministic (defined in this plan), not improvised.
task.acceptance: Every service in docker-compose.flows.yml has a healthcheck directive. Test parses the YAML and asserts all services have healthcheck with test, interval, timeout, retries, and start_period fields. Zero services without healthcheck (excluding hpo-worker which is a scaled replica of hpo and inherits its healthcheck pattern).

task.title: AST enforcement test: healthcheck scripts use only stdlib
task.description:
Write a test that parses healthcheck_training.py and healthcheck_cpu.py with ast.parse() and verifies that NO import statement references the minivess package. Healthcheck scripts must work in ANY container image (including Tier C light images that may not have the full minivess package installed).
Also verify both scripts have a __main__ guard and sys.exit() call.
task.acceptance: Test asserts both healthcheck scripts import only from stdlib modules. No minivess, torch, mlflow, prefect, or any third-party imports.

phase.title: Wire stall detection into dashboard health checks
phase.description:
detect_mlflow_metric_stall() exists in stall_detection.py and is tested in isolation (test_stall_detection.py). But no flow, task, or monitoring loop ever calls it. The dashboard flow is the natural consumer — it already aggregates pipeline status and has a health-check responsibility.
This phase adds a stall detection task to the dashboard flow and optionally wires it into the GpuHeartbeatMonitor as a cross-check signal.

task.title: Add stall detection task to dashboard flow
task.description:
Add a new @task to dashboard_flow.py that discovers active MLflow runs and checks each for metric staleness:
@task(name="check-training-stall", on_completion=[_on_complete], on_failure=[_on_fail]) def check_training_stall_task( *, tracking_uri: str, threshold_minutes: float = 30.0, ) -> dict[str, Any]: """Check active MLflow runs for metric staleness.
Returns dict with 'stale_runs' list and 'total_active' count. """ import mlflow
mlflow.set_tracking_uri(tracking_uri) client = mlflow.MlflowClient()
# Find runs with status RUNNING from mlflow.entities import ViewType active_runs = client.search_runs( experiment_ids=["0"], # Default experiment filter_string="status = 'RUNNING'", run_view_type=ViewType.ACTIVE_ONLY, max_results=50, )
stale_runs = [] for run in active_runs: result = detect_mlflow_metric_stall( run_id=run.info.run_id, threshold_minutes=threshold_minutes, tracking_uri=tracking_uri, ) if result.stale: stale_runs.append({ "run_id": run.info.run_id[:8], "minutes_since": result.minutes_since, "message": result.message, })
return { "total_active": len(active_runs), "stale_runs": stale_runs, "has_stale": len(stale_runs) > 0, }
Wire this task into the run_dashboard_flow() body, after the existing sections are collected. The stall check result is included in the dashboard metadata JSON output.
The threshold_minutes comes from the STALL_THRESHOLD_MINUTES env var (already in x-common-env, default 30).
task.acceptance: Dashboard flow includes stall detection task. When called with a tracking URI that has active runs, it reports stale/fresh status. Test mocks MLflow client to return runs with old timestamps and verifies stale detection.

task.title: Add MLflow run_id callback to GpuHeartbeatMonitor
task.description:
GpuHeartbeatMonitor currently only checks GPU utilization. Add an optional set_mlflow_run_id() method that enables cross-referencing with MLflow stall detection. When both low GPU utilization AND MLflow metric staleness are detected simultaneously, emit a combined diagnostic event.
class GpuHeartbeatMonitor: def set_mlflow_run_id(self, run_id: str, tracking_uri: str) -> None: """Enable MLflow metric stall cross-check.
When GPU utilization is low AND metrics are stale, the combined signal is much stronger than either alone (reduces false positives from data loading pauses or checkpoint saving). """ self._mlflow_run_id = run_id self._mlflow_tracking_uri = tracking_uri
def _check_low_utilization(self, snapshot: dict[str, Any]) -> None: # ... existing logic ... # NEW: if low util AND mlflow_run_id set, cross-check if (util < self._low_util_threshold_pct and self._mlflow_run_id and now - self._low_util_start > self._alert_after_s): stall = detect_mlflow_metric_stall( self._mlflow_run_id, threshold_minutes=self._alert_after_s / 60.0, tracking_uri=self._mlflow_tracking_uri, ) if stall.stale: logger.error( "COMBINED STALL: GPU util=%d%% for %.0fs " "AND MLflow metrics stale for %.1f min. " "Training is likely stuck.", util, duration, stall.minutes_since, )
The set_mlflow_run_id() call happens in train_flow.py after the MLflow run starts. This is optional — the heartbeat monitor works fine without it (graceful degradation).
task.acceptance: GpuHeartbeatMonitor with set_mlflow_run_id() detects combined stall (low GPU + stale MLflow metrics) and logs ERROR with both signals. Without set_mlflow_run_id(), behavior is identical to pass 3.

phase.title: OTel trace propagation into flow context managers
phase.description:
TelemetryProvider (telemetry.py) creates OTel spans via start_as_current_span but is never instantiated inside flow_observability_context or gpu_flow_observability_context. The OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT env var is already in x-common-env (docker-compose.flows.yml line 63) and .env.example (line 130), but no Python code reads it.
This phase wires TelemetryProvider into the flow context managers with graceful degradation: OTel spans are created when the endpoint is configured, silently skipped when not. opentelemetry packages are already in pyproject.toml (lines 57-58) as core dependencies.

task.title: Wire TelemetryProvider into flow_observability_context
task.description:
Modify flow_observability_context() in flow_observability.py to optionally create OTel spans:
1. Check os.environ.get("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT") 2. If set and non-empty, try to instantiate TelemetryProvider 3. Wrap the flow body in a span named "flow/{flow_name}" 4. Add flow_name, status, and duration_s as span attributes on exit 5. Catch ImportError gracefully (opentelemetry may not be installed in Tier C light images) 6. If OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT is empty or unset, skip OTel entirely
Implementation pattern:
@contextlib.contextmanager def flow_observability_context( flow_name: str, *, logs_dir: Path | None = None, ) -> Generator[StructuredEventLogger, None, None]: # ... existing setup ...
# OTel tracing (graceful degradation) otel_span = None otel_endpoint = os.environ.get("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT", "") if otel_endpoint: try: from minivess.observability.telemetry import TelemetryProvider tp = TelemetryProvider( service_name=f"minivess-{flow_name}", endpoint=otel_endpoint, ) otel_span = tp.tracer.start_span( f"flow/{flow_name}", attributes={"flow.name": flow_name}, ) except (ImportError, Exception): logger.debug("OTel not available — tracing disabled")
try: yield event_logger finally: # ... existing cleanup ... if otel_span is not None: otel_span.set_attribute("flow.status", status) otel_span.set_attribute("flow.duration_s", elapsed) otel_span.end()
Apply the same pattern to gpu_flow_observability_context(). Since it delegates to flow_observability_context via `with flow_observability_context(...)`, the OTel span is automatically created — no additional code needed.
task.acceptance: When OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT is set, flow_observability_context creates OTel spans with flow name, status, and duration attributes. When unset/empty, no OTel code runs. ImportError is caught gracefully. Existing JSONL logging is unaffected.

task.title: Add OTel child spans to Prefect task hooks
task.description:
Extend create_task_timing_hooks() in prefect_hooks.py to optionally create OTel child spans for each task execution. This gives per-task trace granularity inside flow spans.
Strategy: Accept an optional tracer parameter. If provided, each hook creates a child span with task_name, duration_s, and status attributes. If not provided, hooks work exactly as before (Python logger only).
def create_task_timing_hooks( *, tracer: Any | None = None, ) -> tuple[Any, Any]: def on_completion(task: Any, task_run: Any, state: Any) -> None: task_name = getattr(task, "name", "unknown") duration_s = _compute_duration(task_run) logger.info(...)
# OTel child span (optional) if tracer is not None: with tracer.start_as_current_span( f"task/{task_name}", attributes={ "task.name": task_name, "task.status": "completed", "task.duration_s": duration_s, }, ): pass # Span auto-closes
# Similar for on_failure...
The tracer is set at module level in each flow file when OTel is active. Since flow files already define _on_complete, _on_fail at module level via create_task_timing_hooks(), we need a way to inject the tracer AFTER module load (since OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT is a runtime check).
Preferred approach: Make the hooks check for a module-level _otel_tracer variable that is set by the flow body at runtime when OTel is active. This avoids changing all 15 flow files — the hooks self-discover.
task.acceptance: When a tracer is provided, each task completion/failure creates a child OTel span. Span hierarchy: flow/train > task/train-one-fold > task/log-fold-results. When no tracer, hooks work as before.

phase.title: Dashboard as observability consumer
phase.description:
The dashboard flow collects data, config, model, and pipeline sections but has no observability section. It should also aggregate: - Training status from heartbeat.json (is training running? which epoch?) - Flow progress from events.jsonl (which flows completed? durations?) - Stall detection results (from Phase 3.1 task) - GPU metrics summary (if available)
This makes the dashboard flow the single pane of glass for pipeline health, complementing Grafana (which requires the monitoring Docker profile).

task.title: Add ObservabilityDashboardSection to dashboard sections
task.acceptance: ObservabilityDashboardSection dataclass exists with all fields. EverythingDashboard accepts optional observability field.

task.title: Add observability collection task to dashboard flow
task.description:
Add a new @task that reads heartbeat.json and events.jsonl from the shared logs volume to build ObservabilityDashboardSection:
@task(name="collect-observability-section", on_completion=[_on_complete], on_failure=[_on_fail]) def collect_observability_section_task( *, logs_dir: Path, stall_result: dict[str, Any] | None = None, ) -> ObservabilityDashboardSection: """Read heartbeat + events for observability status.""" heartbeat_path = logs_dir / "heartbeat.json" events_path = logs_dir / "events.jsonl"
# Read heartbeat training_active = False current_epoch = None max_epochs = None current_val_dice = None eta_s = None heartbeat_age_s = None gpu_status = "no_data"
if heartbeat_path.exists(): hb = json.loads(heartbeat_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")) heartbeat_ts = datetime.fromisoformat(hb["timestamp"]) heartbeat_age_s = (datetime.now(UTC) - heartbeat_ts).total_seconds() training_active = heartbeat_age_s < 300 # 5 min threshold current_epoch = hb.get("epoch") max_epochs = hb.get("max_epochs") current_val_dice = hb.get("val_dice") eta_s = hb.get("eta_s") gpu_status = hb.get("status", "unknown")
# Read last N events recent_events = [] if events_path.exists(): lines = events_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").strip().split("\n") for line in lines[-20:]: # Last 20 events try: recent_events.append(json.loads(line)) except json.JSONDecodeError: pass
return ObservabilityDashboardSection( training_active=training_active, current_epoch=current_epoch, max_epochs=max_epochs, current_val_dice=current_val_dice, eta_s=eta_s, heartbeat_age_s=heartbeat_age_s, recent_flow_events=recent_events, stall_detection=stall_result or {}, gpu_status=gpu_status, )
Wire this task into run_dashboard_flow() and include the result in the metadata export and markdown report.
task.acceptance: Dashboard flow reads heartbeat.json and events.jsonl from logs volume. Metadata JSON output includes observability section with training status, epoch progress, and stall detection results. Markdown report includes an Observability section.

task.title: Add Observability section to markdown report generator
task.description:
Extend generate_report() in dashboard_flow.py to include an Observability section when the observability data is available:
## Observability
- **Training active:** Yes (epoch 15/100, val_dice=0.82) - **ETA:** ~45 minutes - **Heartbeat age:** 12 seconds - **GPU status:** healthy - **Stale runs:** None - **Recent events:** 3 flow_end, 2 epoch_complete in last hour
This section is omitted when no observability data is available (backward compatible).
task.acceptance: Markdown report includes Observability section with training status, ETA, heartbeat age, GPU status, and stall detection summary. Section is skipped when observability data is None.

phase.title: Behavioral verification tests
phase.description:
Existing observability tests verify code structure (AST enforcement, import checks) and unit behavior (StructuredEventLogger methods work in isolation). Missing: tests that verify the full observability pipeline produces correct artifacts when a flow ACTUALLY RUNS.
These behavioral tests use MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST=1 escape hatch (no Docker) and mock MLflow/Prefect to run in staging tier.

task.title: End-to-end flow observability artifact test
task.description:
Write a test that runs a minimal CPU flow variant (biostatistics or a custom test flow) and verifies:
1. events.jsonl exists in LOGS_DIR with flow_start and flow_end events 2. Events are in chronological order (timestamps monotonically increasing) 3. flow_end has status=completed and duration_s > 0 4. All events have required fields (timestamp, event_type) 5. JSONL is valid (each line parses as JSON)
Uses monkeypatch.setenv to set LOGS_DIR, MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI, MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST, and PREFECT_DISABLED. Runs the flow function directly (not via Prefect deployment).
task.acceptance: Test runs a real flow function and verifies events.jsonl content. Not an AST check — the flow actually executes and produces artifacts. Runs in staging tier (<3 min, no model loading, no Docker).

task.title: Heartbeat freshness behavioral test
task.description:
Write a test that exercises the GPU flow observability context (with mocked CUDA) and verifies heartbeat.json is:
1. Created within 1 second of context entry 2. Updated at least once during a 1-second sleep inside the context 3. Contains required keys: timestamp, gpu_util_pct, gpu_memory_used_mb, status 4. Timestamp is parseable ISO 8601 5. Status is "healthy" or "no_gpu" (depending on GPU availability)
Also verify heartbeat.json STOPS being updated after context exit (the background thread has stopped).
task.acceptance: Test exercises the real GpuHeartbeatMonitor (via gpu_flow_observability_context) and verifies heartbeat.json lifecycle: created, updated, and stopped.

task.title: OTel graceful degradation behavioral test
task.description:
Write a test that verifies:
1. With OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT="" (empty): flow runs without OTel, no ImportError, no connection attempts, events.jsonl still written 2. With OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT unset: same as above 3. With OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT="http://localhost:4317" (set but unreachable): flow runs, OTel initialization is attempted but failure is caught, events.jsonl still written
This prevents the monitoring stack from becoming a hard dependency. Flow execution must NEVER fail because OTel is unavailable.
task.acceptance: All three scenarios pass: OTel off, OTel unset, OTel unreachable. In all cases, the flow completes and events.jsonl is written correctly. Zero exceptions propagated to the flow.

task.title: Healthcheck script behavioral tests
task.description:
Write tests that invoke healthcheck_training.py and healthcheck_cpu.py as subprocess calls (simulating Docker HEALTHCHECK CMD execution) and verify:
1. Fresh heartbeat/events: exit code 0 2. Stale heartbeat/events (timestamp > STALL_THRESHOLD_MINUTES ago): exit code 1 3. Missing heartbeat/events within grace period: exit code 0 4. Missing heartbeat/events after grace period: exit code 1 5. Malformed JSON: exit code 1
Uses subprocess.run() to invoke the scripts directly, matching how Docker HEALTHCHECK CMD would execute them.
task.acceptance: All 5 scenarios pass for both healthcheck scripts. Tests use subprocess to match real Docker HEALTHCHECK execution. Exit codes match expected healthy/unhealthy states.

phase.title: Knowledge graph update for observability domain
phase.description: knowledge-graph/domains/observability.yaml was last reviewed on 2026-03-13 and is missing decisions for all the observability infrastructure added in passes 2, 3, and 4. This phase updates it with new resolved decisions.

task.title: Update observability.yaml with pass 3 and 4 decisions
task.description:
Add the following resolved decisions to observability.yaml:
structured_event_logging: status: resolved winner: jsonl_event_logger rationale: "StructuredEventLogger writes events.jsonl + heartbeat.json. Supplements MLflow (not replaces). Machine-parseable for LLM monitoring." implementation: [src/minivess/observability/structured_logging.py, src/minivess/observability/flow_observability.py]
gpu_heartbeat_monitoring: status: resolved winner: pynvml_background_thread rationale: "GpuHeartbeatMonitor checks GPU util every 30s via pynvml. Writes heartbeat.json. Alerts on sustained low utilization." implementation: [src/minivess/observability/gpu_heartbeat.py]
stall_detection: status: resolved winner: mlflow_metric_staleness rationale: "detect_mlflow_metric_stall() checks MLflow run timestamps. Wired into dashboard flow + GpuHeartbeatMonitor cross-check." implementation: [src/minivess/observability/stall_detection.py, src/minivess/orchestration/flows/dashboard_flow.py]
flow_observability_wiring: status: resolved winner: context_manager_per_flow rationale: "All 15 flows use flow_observability_context or gpu_flow_observability_context. AST enforcement prevents regression." implementation: [src/minivess/observability/flow_observability.py, tests/v2/unit/orchestration/test_flow_observability_enforcement.py]
docker_healthcheck: status: resolved winner: heartbeat_plus_events_staleness rationale: "GPU flows use heartbeat.json staleness (healthcheck_training.py). CPU flows use events.jsonl staleness (healthcheck_cpu.py). HTTP services use curl endpoint checks." implementation: [scripts/healthcheck_training.py, scripts/healthcheck_cpu.py, deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml]
monitoring_stack: status: resolved winner: grafana_lgtm_single_container rationale: "grafana/otel-lgtm provides Grafana + Loki + Tempo + Prometheus in a single container. Activated via --profile observability." implementation: [deployment/docker-compose.yml]
gpu_hardware_metrics: status: resolved winner: dcgm_exporter rationale: "NVIDIA DCGM exporter for hardware-level GPU metrics (temp, power, ECC, NVLink). Complements pynvml heartbeat." implementation: [deployment/docker-compose.yml]
otel_trace_propagation: status: resolved winner: telemetry_provider_in_context rationale: "TelemetryProvider creates OTel spans inside flow_observability_context. Graceful degradation when OTel unavailable." implementation: [src/minivess/observability/flow_observability.py, src/minivess/observability/telemetry.py]
task_timing_hooks: status: resolved winner: prefect_task_hooks rationale: "create_task_timing_hooks() provides on_completion/on_failure hooks for all ~77 @task decorators. AST enforcement prevents regression." implementation: [src/minivess/observability/prefect_hooks.py, tests/v2/unit/orchestration/test_all_flows_observability.py]
Update last_reviewed to "2026-03-29". Add metalearning reference for the silent-cpu-fallback incident. Add planning_docs reference to all four pass plans.
task.acceptance: observability.yaml contains all 9 new decisions with status: resolved. last_reviewed is 2026-03-29. All implementation paths are valid files. Test verifies KG completeness: every observability module has a corresponding decision in the domain YAML.

phase.title: Full verification and staging gate
phase.description: Final verification phase. Run the complete staging test suite and verify all new tests pass alongside the existing 7083+ tests. Fix any failures per CLAUDE.md Rule #23 (batch resolution, never serial whac-a-mole).

task.title: Run make test-staging with --maxfail=200
task.description:
Run the full staging test suite: 1. make test-staging (--maxfail=200, no -x) 2. Verify zero failures in new test files 3. Verify zero new skips introduced 4. Run ruff check on all modified files 5. Run mypy on all modified files
If failures occur, categorize by root cause and fix in batches. Per Rule #23: GATHER → CATEGORIZE → PLAN → FIX → VERIFY.
task.acceptance: make test-staging passes with 0 failures, 0 new skips. ruff and mypy report no errors in modified files. Total test count increases by the number of new test functions (~35-45 expected).

task.title: Verify Docker Compose YAML validity
task.description:
Validate the modified docker-compose.flows.yml: 1. docker compose -f deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml config (YAML valid) 2. Existing compose correctness tests pass 3. All services have healthcheck (new test from Phase 2.2) 4. No new services added (healthcheck only) 5. Cross-reference with yaml_contract.yaml if applicable
NOTE: This does NOT start containers. It validates YAML syntax and schema only. Docker runtime testing is manual (scripts/demo_observability.sh from pass 3).
task.acceptance: docker compose config succeeds. All compose tests pass. All services have healthcheck directives.

key_risks.risk: Phase 1 (trainer threading): SegmentationTrainer.fit() is the most critical code path. Changes must not affect training correctness. Mitigated by: event_logger is optional (None = no-op), and logging happens AFTER train/val results are computed (no side effects on training logic).

Phase 1.2 (threading.local for event_logger): Relies on Prefect running tasks in the same process. If Prefect ever switches to remote workers, the threading.local approach breaks. Mitigated by: Prefect 3.x default for single-machine is process-local; cloud deployments would need different observability anyway.

Phase 2 (Docker Compose healthchecks): Adding healthcheck to all services increases Docker daemon overhead slightly (one healthcheck process per container per interval). With 60s intervals, this is negligible.

Phase 4 (OTel wiring): opentelemetry packages are in pyproject.toml as core deps (lines 57-58). However, Tier C light images may not include them. ImportError is caught gracefully — zero risk to flow execution.

Phase 3.2 (heartbeat stall cross-check): Adds MLflow client call inside the heartbeat background thread. This is rate-limited by the heartbeat interval (30s) and only fires after the alert threshold (120s). Network timeout is bounded. Failure is caught and logged — never blocks training.

summary.dependency_chain: Phase 1 (trainer) and Phase 2 (healthcheck) are independent — can be parallelized. Phase 3 (stall detection) depends on Phase 1 (trainer produces heartbeat.json). Phase 4 (OTel) is independent of Phases 1-3. Phase 5 (dashboard consumer) depends on Phases 1 and 3 (reads their artifacts). Phase 6 (behavioral tests) depends on Phases 1-5 (tests what they produce). Phase 7 (KG update) depends on Phases 1-6 (documents what was built). Phase 8 (verification) depends on ALL previous phases.
